title: The AI wearable that caught my arrhythmia before symptoms appeared
post:
I've been wearing a continuous heart monitor for six months. Not because I had symptoms — because my family has a history of cardiac issues and I wanted data. What I got was a wake-up call.

Three weeks ago, the AI analysis in my wearable flagged a pattern consistent with atrial fibrillation. I had zero symptoms. I felt completely normal. But the data didn't lie. I got it checked out, confirmed the diagnosis, and started treatment before it could progress to something more serious.

This is preventive medicine reimagined. We used to wait for symptoms to tell us something was wrong. Now the data speaks first. My cardiologist told me this kind of early detection simply wasn't possible at this scale even five years ago.

Are you monitoring your health data proactively, or waiting for something to go wrong? Thoughts?
